blocks.html: Intermission Let’s pause for a moment. Up to this point you should have mastered all the basic stuff. In the second half of this book things will get a bit more detail- specific, so it’s worth looking back over what you have learnt. Look through the following key points and ask yourself whether you understand them already. If you do, then you’re safe to move on, but if any of these points makes you scratch your head in total confusion, then why not go back and review them again? Consonants 1. Do you remember all these consonants, their sounds and their classes? (DAY 1 through 4)

items.thai: - ง ม น ร ล ย ว
-ค ท ช ซ พ ฟ ฮ
- ก จ ด ต บ ป อ
- ข ถ ฉ ส ผ ฝ ห

blocks.html: 2. Do you remember that the consonants in each of these line sound the

same at final position? (Final consonant (continued)) -น, -ร, -ล -ค, -ก, -ข -ท, -ช, -ซ, -จ, -ด, -ต, -ถ, -ส -พ, -ฟ, -บ, -ป 3. Do you remember that อ (as a consonant) has no sound of its own, but is used as a placeholder consonant for vowels? (Placeholder “อ”)
- อา, อาน, เอา, ออม 4. Do you remember that ห becomes silent in front of an LC1 consonant and converts that LC1 consonant into a HC instead? (Silent “ห”)

- หรู, หมี, หลัก, ไหน, เหมา, หยาม, หงอก Vowels 1. Do you remember all these vowels, whether they are long or short and whether they can they take a final consonant? (DAY 1 through 5, vowels that don’t take final consonants)

items.thai: - -ำ ไ- ใ- เ-า เ-ย

blocks.html: 2. Do you remember that these vowel pairs are essentially the same vowel
sounds but look different depending on whether there’s a final consonant or not? (Vowels with two forms 1 and 2)

items: -ะ > -ั-, -ือ > -ื-, เ-อ > เ-ิ-, -ัว > -ว- 3.Do you remember that when there are two consonants together with no vowel written anywhere, it’s probably a short [-o-] sound or [-orn] if the

blocks.html: final consonant is ร? (Camouflage Vowels (2 letters))
1. Do you remember the concept of live-dead syllables, and how to identify them? (Live-dead syllables)

items: DEAD syllable: “Chôhk…” 2. Do you remember the tone rules: The String Theory #1 and #2? (Theory of Tones) HC MC LC Live rising mid Dead LongV low falling ShortV high All checked? Then let’s continue! The last two rules of the String Theory: You’ll learn about tone marks and words with mismatch pronunciations, as well as the last three complex- looking vowels and how to identify vowels that start
